revision.text:
Der '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (auch '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') beinhaltet Texte zum [[Nutbuch]], die aus dem zweiten Jahrhundert n. Chr. aus [[Tebtynis]] stammen. Erstmalig wurde der Inhalt aus PC1 im Jahr 1940 veröffentlicht. Eine weitere Veröffentlichung folgte 1960.
Es handelt sich um einen [[Hieratische Schrift|hieratischen Text]] mit [[Demotische Sprache|demotischer Übersetzung]] und demotischen Kommentar. Der Papyrus besteht aus sieben [[Spaltensatz|Kolumnen]], vier [[recto]] und drei [[verso]] mit jeweils 44 Zeichen. PC1 ist bis auf den Anfang weitestgehend erhalten.
Der [[Papyrus (Beschreibstoff)|Papyrus]] hat eine Länge von 67,5 cm, eine Seitenhöhe von etwa 30 cm; die Höhe des Schriftspiegels beträgt 24,8 cm. Die Kolumnenbreite schwankt zwischen 14,3 und 17,2 cm. Die Kolumnen selbst haben folgende Verteilung: recto III = verso V, recto II = verso VI, recto I = verso VII; recto IV steht für sich alleine und hat keinen entsprechenden verso-Eintrag.
Der Text umfasst in demotischer Schrift das Nutbild. Die Dekanlisten fehlen. Der Papyrus endet mit Abbruch nach dem ersten Satz des Mondkapitels.
== Literatur == * Hans-Otto Lange, [[Otto Neugebauer]]: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 - Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text -'', Munksgaard, Kopenhagen 1940 * Otto Neugebauer, [[Richard-Anthony Parker]]: ''[[Otto Neugebauer#Werke|Egyptian astronomical Texts (EAT) - Bd. 1: The early decans -]]'', Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, S. 36-87
[[Kategorie:Papyrus]] [[Kategorie:Archäologischer Fund (Ägypten)]]
[[ca:Papir Carlsberg]] [[en:Carlsberg papyrus]] [[es:Papiro Carlsberg]]

Es handelt sich um einen [[Hieratische Schrift|hieratischen Text]] mit [[Demotische Sprache|demotischer Übersetzung]] und demotischen Kommentar. Der [[Papyrus (Beschreibstoff)|Papyrus]] hat eine Länge von 67,5 cm, eine Seitenhöhe von etwa 30 cm; die Höhe des Schriftspiegels beträgt 24,8 cm. Die Kolumnenbreite schwankt zwischen 14,3 und 17,2 cm. Die Kolumnen selbst haben folgende Verteilung: recto III = verso V, recto II = verso VI, recto I = verso VII; recto IV steht für sich alleine und hat keinen entsprechenden verso-Eintrag.
Der Text umfasst in demotischer Schrift das Nutbild. Die Dekanlisten fehlen. Der Papyrus endet mit Abbruch nach dem ersten Satz des Mondkapitels.

[[Kategorie:Papyrus]] [[Kategorie:Archäologischer Fund (Ägypten)]]

Der '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (auch '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') beinhaltet Texte zum [[Nutbuch]], die aus dem zweiten Jahrhundert n. Chr. aus [[Tebtynis]] stammen. Erstmals wurde der Inhalt aus PC1 im Jahr 1940 veröffentlicht. Eine weitere Veröffentlichung folgte 1960.
Es handelt sich um einen [[Hieratische Schrift|hieratischen Text]] mit [[Demotische Sprache|demotischer Übersetzung]] und demotischem Kommentar. Der [[Papyrus (Beschreibstoff)|Papyrus]] besteht aus sieben [[Spaltensatz|Kolumnen]], vier [[recto]] und drei [[verso]] mit jeweils 44 Zeichen. PC1 ist bis auf den Anfang weitestgehend erhalten.
Der Papyrus hat eine Länge von 67,5 cm, eine Seitenhöhe von etwa 30 cm; die Höhe des Schriftspiegels beträgt 24,8 cm. Die Kolumnenbreite schwankt zwischen 14,3 und 17,2 cm. Die Kolumnen selbst haben folgende Verteilung: recto III = verso V, recto II = verso VI, recto I = verso VII; recto IV steht für sich alleine und hat keinen entsprechenden verso-Eintrag.
Der Text umfasst in demotischer Schrift das Nutbild. Die Dekanlisten fehlen. Der Papyrus endet mit Abbruch nach dem ersten Satz des Mondkapitels.

Der '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (auch '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') beinhaltet Texte zum [[Nutbuch]], die aus dem zweiten Jahrhundert n. Chr. aus [[Tebtynis]] stammen. Er befindet sich heute im Ägyptologischen Institut der [[Universität Kopenhagen]]. Erstmals wurde der Inhalt aus PC1 im Jahr 1940 veröffentlicht. Eine weitere Veröffentlichung folgte 1960.
Es handelt sich um einen [[Hieratische Schrift|hieratischen Text]] mit [[Demotische Sprache|demotischer Übersetzung]] und demotischem Kommentar. Der [[Papyrus]] besteht aus sieben [[Spaltensatz|Kolumnen]], vier [[recto]] und drei [[verso]] mit jeweils 44 Zeichen. PC1 ist bis auf den Anfang weitestgehend erhalten.
Der Papyrus hat eine Länge von 67,5 cm, eine Seitenhöhe von etwa 30 cm; die Höhe des Schriftspiegels beträgt 24,8 cm. Die Kolumnenbreite schwankt zwischen 14,3 und 17,2 cm. Die Kolumnen selbst haben folgende Verteilung: recto III = verso V, recto II = verso VI, recto I = verso VII; recto IV steht für sich alleine und hat keinen entsprechenden verso-Eintrag.
Der Text umfasst in demotischer Schrift das Nutbild. Die Dekanlisten fehlen. Der Papyrus endet mit Abbruch nach dem ersten Satz des Mondkapitels.

It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the {{ill|University of Copenhagen|de|Universität Kopenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン大学}}. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publica..."
revision.text:
The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書}}, which date from the second century A.D. and originate from {{ill|Tebtynis|de|Tebtynis|ja|テブティニス}}. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the {{ill|University of Copenhagen|de|Universität Kopenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン大学}}. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a {{ill|Hieratic script|de|Hieratische Schrift|ja|ヒエラティック文字|lt=hieratic text|lt_de=hieratischen Text|lt_ja=ヒエラティックテキスト}} featuring a {{ill|Demotic language|de|Demotische Sprache|ja|デモティック言語|lt=demotic translation|lt_de=demotischer Übersetzung|lt_ja=デモティック翻訳}} and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|ja|パピルス}} consists of seven {{ill|column layout|de|Spaltensatz|ja|列組|lt=columns|lt_de=Kolumnen|lt_ja=列}}, four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
== See also == * {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja|カルルスベルグパピルス1a}}
== Literature == * Hans-Otto Lange, {{ill|Otto Neugebauer|de|Otto Neugebauer|ja|オットー・ノイゲバウアー}}: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text.'' Munksgaard, Copenhagen 1940. * {{ill|Otto Neugebauer|de|Otto Neugebauer|ja|オットー・ノイゲバウアー}}, {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌}}.'' (EAT), Volume 1: ''The early decans.'' Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, pp. 36–87.

It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the {{ill|University of Copenhagen|de|Universität Kopenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン大学}}. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a {{ill|Hieratic script|de|Hieratische Schrift|ja|ヒエラティック文字|lt=hieratic text|lt_de=hieratischen Text|lt_ja=ヒエラティックテキスト}} featuring a {{ill|Demotic language|de|Demotische Sprache|ja|デモティック言語|lt=demotic translation|lt_de=demotischer Übersetzung|lt_ja=デモティック翻訳}} and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|ja|パピルス}} consists of seven {{ill|column layout|de|Spaltensatz|ja|列組|lt=columns|lt_de=Kolumnen|lt_ja=列}}, four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.

The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.

revision.text:
The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書}}, which date from the second century A.D. and originate from {{ill|Tebtynis|de|Tebtynis|ja|テブティニス}}. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the {{ill|University of Copenhagen|de|Universität Kopenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン大学}}. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a {{ill|Hieratic script|de|Hieratische Schrift|ja|ヒエラティック文字|lt=hieratic text|lt_de=hieratischen Text|lt_ja=ヒエラティックテキスト}} featuring a {{ill|Demotic language|de|Demotische Sprache|ja|デモティック言語|lt=demotic translation|lt_de=demotischer Übersetzung|lt_ja=デモティック翻訳}} and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|ja|パピルス}} consists of seven {{ill|column layout|de|Spaltensatz|ja|列組|lt=columns|lt_de=Kolumnen|lt_ja=列}}, four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.

revision.text:
The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|12=draft}}, which date from the second century A.D. and originate from {{ill|Tebtynis|de|Tebtynis|ja|テブティニス|12=draft}}. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the {{ill|University of Copenhagen|de|Universität Kopenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン大学|12=draft}}. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a {{ill|Hieratic script|de|Hieratische Schrift|ja|ヒエラティック文字|lt=hieratic text|lt_de=hieratischen Text|lt_ja=ヒエラティックテキスト|12=draft}} featuring a {{ill|Demotic language|de|Demotische Sprache|ja|デモティック言語|lt=demotic translation|lt_de=demotischer Übersetzung|lt_ja=デモティック翻訳|12=draft}} and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|ja|パピルス|12=draft}} consists of seven {{ill|column layout|de|Spaltensatz|ja|列組|lt=columns|lt_de=Kolumnen|lt_ja=列|12=draft}}, four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|12=draft}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|12=draft}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
== See also == * {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja|カルルスベルグパピルス1a|12=draft}}
== Literature == * Hans-Otto Lange, {{ill|Otto Neugebauer|de|Otto Neugebauer|ja|オットー・ノイゲバウアー|12=draft}}: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text.'' Munksgaard, Copenhagen 1940. * {{ill|Otto Neugebauer|de|Otto Neugebauer|ja|オットー・ノイゲバウアー|12=draft}}, {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー|12=draft}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌|12=draft}}.'' (EAT), Volume 1: ''The early decans.'' Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, pp. 36–87.
== References == <references/>
{{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Papyrus]] [[Category:Archaeological Find (Egypt)]] [[Category:University of Copenhagen]] [[Category:Archaeological Find (Africa)]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:パピルス・カルルスベルグ1]] [[de:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]] [[sn:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]]

It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the {{ill|University of Copenhagen|de|Universität Kopenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン大学|12=draft}}. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.

The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.

The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|qq=}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|qq=}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.

revision.text:
The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|qq=}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|qq=}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
== See also == * {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja|カルルスベルグパピルス1a|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|qq=}}
== Literature == * Hans-Otto Lange, [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]]: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text.'' Munksgaard, Copenhagen 1940. * [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]], {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard-Anthony Parker|qq=}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian astronomical Texts|qq=}}.'' (EAT), Volume 1: ''The early decans.'' Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, pp. 36–87.
== References == <references/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|qq=}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|qq=}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
== See also == * {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja|カルルスベルグパピルス1a|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Literature == * Hans-Otto Lange, [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]]: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text.'' Munksgaard, Copenhagen 1940. * [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]], {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard-Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian astronomical Texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.'' (EAT), Volume 1: ''The early decans.'' Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, pp. 36–87.
== References == <references/>
{{translated page|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1|version=243749929|comment=Imported full de history}}

[[Category:University of Copenhagen]]
[[Category:Completion tier 1]]
[[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q25377583]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|qq=|1=recto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626422}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|qq=|1=verso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626717}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.

Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]], {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard-Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian astronomical Texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.'' <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q25377583]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|1=recto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626422}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|1=verso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626717}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
== See also == * {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja|カルルスベルグパピルス1a|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Literature == * Hans-Otto Lange, [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]]: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text.'' Munksgaard, Copenhagen 1940. * [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]], {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard-Anthony Parker|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.'' (EAT), Volume 1: ''The early decans.'' Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, pp. 36–87.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q25377583]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|1=recto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626422}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|1=verso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626717}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Papyrus Carlsberg 1]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q25377583]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} The '''Papyrus Carlsberg 1''' (also '''pCarlsberg 1, PC1''') contains texts concerning the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which date from the second century A.D. and originate from [[:en:Tebtunis|Tebtynis]]. It is currently housed at the Egyptological Institute of the [[:en:University of Copenhagen|University of Copenhagen]]. The content from PC1 was first published in 1940, with a further publication following in 1960.
It is a [[:en:Hieratic|hieratic text]] featuring a [[:en:Demotic Egyptian language|demotic translation]] and accompanied by a demotic commentary. The [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] consists of seven [[:en:Column (typography)|columns]], four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|1=recto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626422}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|1=verso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626717}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
== See also == * {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja|カルルスベルグパピルス1a|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1a|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Literature == * Hans-Otto Lange, [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]]: ''Papyrus Carlsberg No. 1 Ein hieratisch-demotischer kosmologischer Text.'' Munksgaard, Copenhagen 1940. * [[:en:Otto E. Neugebauer|Otto Neugebauer]], {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード＝アンソニー・パーカー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard-Anthony Parker|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''{{ill|Egyptian astronomical Texts|de|Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja|エジプト天文学テキスト|lt=Otto Neugebauer Bibliography|lt_de=Otto Neugebauer Bibliographie|lt_ja=オットー・ノイゲバウアーの書誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian astronomical Texts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.'' (EAT), Volume 1: ''The early decans.'' Brown University Press, Rhode Island 1960, pp. 36–87.
== References == <references/>

The {{ill|Papyrus|en|Papyrus|WD=Q125576|lt=Papyrus|1=Papyrus}} consists of seven {{ill|Column (typography)|en|Column (typography)|WD=Q2306599|lt=columns|1=Column (typography)}}, four {{ill|recto|de|recto|ja|表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/recto|1=Q11626422|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626422}} and three {{ill|verso|de|verso|ja|裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/verso|1=Q11626717|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626717}}, each containing 44 characters. PC1 is largely preserved apart from its beginning.
The papyrus measures 67.5 cm in length, with a page height of about 30 cm; the height of the writing mirror is 24.8 cm. The column width varies between 14.3 and 17.2 cm. The columns are arranged as follows: recto III corresponds to verso V, recto II corresponds to verso VI, and recto I corresponds to verso VII; recto IV stands alone and has no corresponding verso entry.
The text, written in demotic script, comprises the Nut depiction. The decan lists are missing. The papyrus ends abruptly after the first sentence of the lunar chapter.
